title: NeuroMorph: A High-Temporal Resolution MEG Dataset for Morpheme-Based Linguistic Analysis

funding_references.funder_name: New York University Abu Dhabi Institute

description: NeuroMorph is a high-temporal resolution magnetoencephalography (MEG) dataset comprising recordings from 24 subjects totaling over 17 hours of data acquired using a KIT/Yokogawa system with 208 magnetometer channels. The dataset is designed to support morpheme-based linguistic analysis and investigation of language and cognitive processes through a visual presentation paradigm. Subjects were presented with visual stimuli to investigate morphological processing and related cognitive mechanisms.
methods_description: Data were collected using a KIT/Yokogawa MEG system with 208 magnetometer channels. The study employed a visual presentation paradigm to investigate language and cognitive processes, specifically targeting morpheme-based linguistic analysis. Subjects viewed visual stimuli while MEG activity was recorded. The dataset includes raw MEG recordings with associated metadata and stimulus information.
